paragraph: Single-family homes in Mahwah are moving in a real seller's market, with 58 percent of homes selling above asking and a median sale-to-list ratio of 1.04. The median price sits at $938,000, and homes are spending a median of 22 days on market before going under contract. Condos and townhouses tell a different story with stronger velocity. They're the volume tier at 86 sales over six months with a median of $488,000, moving even faster at 15 days on market and 67 percent selling above asking. The condo-townhouse sale-to-list ratio of 1.02 shows steady, competitive demand without the waiting game you're seeing in single-family. If you're weighing tiers, the choice here comes down to what you need. Single-family offers space and grounds but requires patience and competitive bidding. Condos and townhouses move faster with lower entry prices, though they come with HOA fees and shared walls. The market isn't favoring one over the other in terms of heat. It's rewarding prepared buyers in both categories who know their budget and move quickly.
